NKJV.1: Therefore, leaving the discussion of the elementary principles of Christ, let us go on to perfection, not laying again the foundation of repentance from dead works and of faith toward God,
NKJV.2: of the doctrine of baptisms, of laying on of hands, of resurrection of the dead, and of eternal judgment.
NKJV.3: And this we will do if God permits.
NKJV.4: For it is impossible for those who were once enlightened, and have tasted the heavenly gift, and have become partakers of the Holy Spirit,
NKJV.5: and have tasted the good word of God and the powers of the age to come,
NKJV.6: if they fall away, to renew them again to repentance, since they crucify again for themselves the Son of God, and put Him to an open shame.
NKJV.7: For the earth which drinks in the rain that often comes upon it, and bears herbs useful for those by whom it is cultivated, receives blessing from God;
NKJV.8: but if it bears thorns and briers, it is rejected and near to being cursed, whose end is to be burned.
NKJV.9: But, beloved, we are confident of better things concerning you, yes, things that accompany salvation, though we speak in this manner.
NKJV.10: For God is not unjust to forget your work and labor of love which you have shown toward His name, in that you have ministered to the saints, and do minister.
NKJV.11: And we desire that each one of you show the same diligence to the full assurance of hope until the end,
NKJV.12: that you do not become sluggish, but imitate those who through faith and patience inherit the promises.
NKJV.13: For when God made a promise to Abraham, because He could swear by no one greater, He swore by Himself,
NKJV.14: saying, “Surely blessing I will bless you, and multiplying I will multiply you.”
NKJV.15: And so, after he had patiently endured, he obtained the promise.
NKJV.16: For men indeed swear by the greater, and an oath for confirmation is for them an end of all dispute.
NKJV.17: Thus God, determining to show more abundantly to the heirs of promise the immutability of His counsel, confirmed it by an oath,
NKJV.18: that by two immutable things, in which it is impossible for God to lie, we might have strong consolation, who have fled for refuge to lay hold of the hope set before us.
NKJV.19: This hope we have as an anchor of the soul, both sure and steadfast, and which enters the Presence behind the veil,
NKJV.20: where the forerunner has entered for us, even Jesus, having become High Priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek.

ESV.1: Therefore let us leave the elementary doctrine of Christ and go on to maturity, not laying again a foundation of repentance from dead works and of faith toward God,
ESV.2: and of instruction about washings,  the laying on of hands, the resurrection of the dead, and eternal judgment.
ESV.3: And this we will do if God permits.
ESV.4: For it is impossible, in the case of those who have once been enlightened, who have tasted the heavenly gift, and have shared in the Holy Spirit,
ESV.5: and have tasted the goodness of the word of God and the powers of the age to come,
ESV.6: and then have fallen away, to restore them again to repentance, since they are crucifying once again the Son of God to their own harm and holding him up to contempt.
ESV.7: For land that has drunk the rain that often falls on it, and produces a crop useful to those for whose sake it is cultivated, receives a blessing from God.
ESV.8: But if it bears thorns and thistles, it is worthless and near to being cursed, and its end is to be burned.
ESV.9: Though we speak in this way, yet in your case, beloved, we feel sure of better things — things that belong to salvation.
ESV.10: For God is not unjust so as to overlook your work and the love that you have shown for his name in serving the saints, as you still do.
ESV.11: And we desire each one of you to show the same earnestness to have the full assurance of hope until the end,
ESV.12: so that you may not be sluggish, but imitators of those who through faith and patience inherit the promises.
ESV.13: For when God made a promise to Abraham, since he had no one greater by whom to swear, he swore by himself,
ESV.14: saying, “Surely I will bless you and multiply you.”
ESV.15: And thus Abraham,  having patiently waited, obtained the promise.
ESV.16: For people swear by something greater than themselves, and in all their disputes an oath is final for confirmation.
ESV.17: So when God desired to show more convincingly to the heirs of the promise the unchangeable character of his purpose, he guaranteed it with an oath,
ESV.18: so that by two unchangeable things, in which it is impossible for God to lie, we who have fled for refuge might have strong encouragement to hold fast to the hope set before us.
ESV.19: We have this as a sure and steadfast anchor of the soul, a hope that enters into the inner place behind the curtain,
ESV.20: where Jesus has gone as a forerunner on our behalf, having become a high priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.

NASB.1: Therefore leaving the elementary teaching about the Christ, let us press on to maturity, not laying again a foundation of repentance from dead works and of faith toward God,
NASB.2: of instruction about washings and laying on of hands, and the resurrection of the dead and eternal judgment.
NASB.3: And this we will do, if God permits.
NASB.4: For in the case of those who have once been enlightened and have tasted of the heavenly gift and have been made partakers of the Holy Spirit,
NASB.5: and have tasted the good word of God and the powers of the age to come,
NASB.6: and [then] have fallen away, it is impossible to renew them again to repentance, since they again crucify to themselves the Son of God and put Him to open shame.
NASB.7: For ground that drinks the rain which often falls on it and brings forth vegetation useful to those for whose sake it is also tilled, receives a blessing from God;
NASB.8: but if it yields thorns and thistles, it is worthless and close to being cursed, and it ends up being burned.
NASB.9: But, beloved, we are convinced of better things concerning you, and things that accompany salvation, though we are speaking in this way.
NASB.10: For God is not unjust so as to forget your work and the love which you have shown toward His name, in having ministered and in still ministering to the saints.
NASB.11: And we desire that each one of you show the same diligence so as to realize the full assurance of hope until the end,
NASB.12: so that you will not be sluggish, but imitators of those who through faith and patience inherit the promises.
NASB.13: For when God made the promise to Abraham, since He could swear by no one greater, He swore by Himself,
NASB.14: saying, 'I WILL SURELY BLESS YOU AND I WILL SURELY MULTIPLY YOU.'
NASB.15: And so, having patiently waited, he obtained the promise.
NASB.16: For men swear by one greater [than themselves], and with them an oath [given] as confirmation is an end of every dispute.
NASB.17: In the same way God, desiring even more to show to the heirs of the promise the unchangeableness of His purpose, interposed with an oath,
NASB.18: so that by two unchangeable things in which it is impossible for God to lie, we who have taken refuge would have strong encouragement to take hold of the hope set before us.
NASB.19: This hope we have as an anchor of the soul, a [hope] both sure and steadfast and one which enters within the veil,
NASB.20: where Jesus has entered as a forerunner for us, having become a high priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek.

NLT.1: So let us stop going over the basic teachings about Christ again and again. Let us go on instead and become mature in our understanding. Surely we don’t need to start again with the fundamental importance of repenting from evil deeds and placing our faith in God.
NLT.2: You don’t need further instruction about baptisms, the laying on of hands, the resurrection of the dead, and eternal judgment.
NLT.3: And so, God willing, we will move forward to further understanding.
NLT.4: For it is impossible to bring back to repentance those who were once enlightened — those who have experienced the good things of heaven and shared in the Holy Spirit,
NLT.5: who have tasted the goodness of the word of God and the power of the age to come —
NLT.6: and who then turn away from God. It is impossible to bring such people back to repentance; by rejecting the Son of God, they themselves are nailing him to the cross once again and holding him up to public shame.
NLT.7: When the ground soaks up the falling rain and bears a good crop for the farmer, it has God’s blessing.
NLT.8: But if a field bears thorns and thistles, it is useless. The farmer will soon condemn that field and burn it.
NLT.9: Dear friends, even though we are talking this way, we really don’t believe it applies to you. We are confident that you are meant for better things, things that come with salvation.
NLT.10: For God is not unjust. He will not forget how hard you have worked for him and how you have shown your love to him by caring for other believers, as you still do.
NLT.11: Our great desire is that you will keep on loving others as long as life lasts, in order to make certain that what you hope for will come true.
NLT.12: Then you will not become spiritually dull and indifferent. Instead, you will follow the example of those who are going to inherit God’s promises because of their faith and endurance.
NLT.13: For example, there was God’s promise to Abraham. Since there was no one greater to swear by, God took an oath in his own name, saying:
NLT.14:
“I will certainly bless you,
and I will multiply your descendants beyond number.”
NLT.15: Then Abraham waited patiently, and he received what God had promised.
NLT.16: Now when people take an oath, they call on someone greater than themselves to hold them to it. And without any question that oath is binding.
NLT.17: God also bound himself with an oath, so that those who received the promise could be perfectly sure that he would never change his mind.
NLT.18: So God has given both his promise and his oath. These two things are unchangeable because it is impossible for God to lie. Therefore, we who have fled to him for refuge can have great confidence as we hold to the hope that lies before us.
NLT.19: This hope is a strong and trustworthy anchor for our souls. It leads us through the curtain into God’s inner sanctuary.
NLT.20: Jesus has already gone in there for us. He has become our eternal High Priest in the order of Melchizedek.

WEB.1: Therefore leaving the teaching of the first principles of Christ, let us press on to perfection — not laying again a foundation of repentance from dead works, of faith toward God,
WEB.2: of the teaching of baptisms, of laying on of hands, of resurrection of the dead, and of eternal judgment.
WEB.3: This will we do, if God permits.
WEB.4: For concerning those who were once enlightened and tasted of the heavenly gift, and were made partakers of the Holy Spirit,
WEB.5: and tasted the good word of God, and the powers of the age to come,
WEB.6: and then fell away, it is impossible to renew them again to repentance; seeing they crucify the Son of God for themselves again, and put him to open shame.
WEB.7: For the land which has drunk the rain that comes often on it, and produces a crop suitable for them for whose sake it is also tilled, receives blessing from God;
WEB.8: but if it bears thorns and thistles, it is rejected and near being cursed, whose end is to be burned.
WEB.9: But, beloved, we are persuaded of better things for you, and things that accompany salvation, even though we speak like this.
WEB.10: For God is not unrighteous, so as to forget your work and the labor of love which you showed toward his name, in that you served the saints, and still do serve them.
WEB.11: We desire that each one of you may show the same diligence to the fullness of hope even to the end,
WEB.12: that you won’t be sluggish, but imitators of those who through faith and perseverance inherited the promises.
WEB.13: For when God made a promise to Abraham, since he could swear by no one greater, he swore by himself,
WEB.14: saying, “Surely blessing I will bless you, and multiplying I will multiply you.”
WEB.15: Thus, having patiently endured, he obtained the promise.
WEB.16: For men indeed swear by a greater one, and in every dispute of theirs the oath is final for confirmation.
WEB.17: In this way God, being determined to show more abundantly to the heirs of the promise the immutability of his counsel, interposed with an oath;
WEB.18: that by two immutable things, in which it is impossible for God to lie, we may have a strong encouragement, who have fled for refuge to take hold of the hope set before us.
WEB.19: This hope we have as an anchor of the soul, a hope both sure and steadfast and entering into that which is within the veil;
WEB.20: where as a forerunner Jesus entered for us, having become a high priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.

CSB17.1: Therefore, let us leave the elementary teaching about Christ and go on to maturity, not laying again a foundation of repentance from dead works, faith in God, <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.2: teaching about ritual washings, <sup>[1]</sup> laying on of hands, the resurrection of the dead, and eternal judgment. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.3: And we will do this if God permits.
CSB17.4: For it is impossible to renew to repentance those who were once enlightened, who tasted the heavenly gift, who shared in the Holy Spirit, <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.5: who tasted God’s good word and the powers of the coming age,
CSB17.6: and who have fallen away. This is because, <sup>[2]</sup> to their own harm, they are recrucifying the Son of God and holding him up to contempt. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.7: For the ground that drinks the rain that often falls on it and that produces vegetation useful to those for whom it is cultivated receives a blessing from God.
CSB17.8: But if it produces thorns and thistles, it is worthless and about to be cursed, and at the end will be burned. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.9: Even though we are speaking this way, dearly loved friends, in your case we are confident of things that are better and that pertain to salvation.
CSB17.10: For God is not unjust; he will not forget your work and the love <sup>[3]</sup> you demonstrated for his name by serving the saints — and by continuing to serve them. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.11: Now we desire each of you to demonstrate the same diligence for the full assurance of your hope until the end,
CSB17.12: so that you won’t become lazy but will be imitators of those who inherit the promises through faith and perseverance. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.13: For when God made a promise to Abraham, since he had no one greater to swear by, he swore by himself:
CSB17.14: I will indeed bless you, and I will greatly multiply you. [4] ⓚ
CSB17.15: And so, after waiting patiently, Abraham obtained the promise.
CSB17.16: For people swear by something greater than themselves, and for them a confirming oath ends every dispute.
CSB17.17: Because God wanted to show his unchangeable purpose even more clearly to the heirs of the promise, he guaranteed it with an oath, <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.18: so that through two unchangeable things, in which it is impossible for God to lie, we who have fled for refuge might have strong encouragement to seize the hope set before us. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.19: We have this hope as an anchor for the soul, firm and secure. It enters the inner sanctuary behind the curtain. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.20: Jesus has entered there on our behalf as a forerunner, <sup>ⓐ</sup> because he has become a high priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek. <sup>ⓑ</sup>

AMP.1: Therefore let us get past the elementary stage in the teachings about the Christ, advancing on to maturity and perfection and spiritual completeness, [doing this] without laying again a foundation of repentance from dead works and of faith toward God,
AMP.2: of teaching about washings (ritual purifications), the laying on of hands, the resurrection of the dead, and eternal judgment. [These are all important matters in which you should have been proficient long ago.]
AMP.3: And we will do this [that is, proceed to maturity], if God permits.
AMP.4: For [it is impossible to restore to repentance] those who have once been enlightened [spiritually] and who have tasted and consciously experienced the heavenly gift and have shared in the Holy Spirit,
AMP.5: and have tasted and consciously experienced the good word of God and the powers of the age (world) to come,
AMP.6: and then have fallen away—it is impossible to bring them back again to repentance, since they again nail the Son of God on the cross [for as far as they are concerned, they are treating the death of Christ as if they were not saved by it], and are holding Him up again to public disgrace.
AMP.7: For soil that drinks the rain which often falls on it and produces crops useful to those for whose benefit it is cultivated, receives a blessing from God;
AMP.8: but if it persistently produces thorns and thistles, it is worthless and close to being cursed, and it ends up being burned.
AMP.9: But, beloved, even though we speak to you in this way, we are convinced of better things concerning you, and of things that accompany salvation.
AMP.10: For God is not unjust so as to forget your work and the love which you have shown for His name in ministering to [the needs of] the saints (God's people), as you do.
AMP.11: And we desire for each one of you to show the same diligence [all the way through] so as to realize and enjoy the full assurance of hope until the end,
AMP.12: so that you will not be [spiritually] sluggish, but [will instead be] imitators of those who through faith [lean on God with absolute trust and confidence in Him and in His power] and by patient endurance [even when suffering] are [now] inheriting the promises.
AMP.13: For when God made the promise to Abraham, He swore [an oath] by Himself, since He had no one greater by whom to swear,
AMP.14: saying, "<e>I will surely bless you and I will surely multiply you</e>."
AMP.15: And so, having patiently waited, he realized the promise [in the miraculous birth of Isaac, as a pledge of what was to come from God].
AMP.16: Indeed men swear [an oath] by one greater than themselves, and with them [in all disputes] the oath serves as confirmation [of what has been said] and is an end of the dispute.
AMP.17: In the same way God, in His desire to show to the heirs of the promise the unchangeable nature of His purpose, intervened and guaranteed it with an oath,
AMP.18: so that by two unchangeable things [His promise and His oath] in which it is impossible for God to lie, we who have fled [to Him] for refuge would have strong encouragement and indwelling strength to hold tightly to the hope set before us.
AMP.19: This hope [this confident assurance] we have as an anchor of the soul [it cannot slip and it cannot break down under whatever pressure bears upon it]—a safe and steadfast hope that enters within the veil [of the heavenly temple, that most Holy Place in which the very presence of God dwells],
AMP.20: where Jesus has entered [in advance] as a forerunner for us, having become a High Priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek.

MSG.1: So come on, let's leave the preschool fingerpainting exercises on Christ and get on with the grand work of art. Grow up in Christ. The basic foundational truths are in place: turning your back on "salvation by self-help" and turning in trust toward God;
MSG.2: baptismal instructions; laying on of hands; resurrection of the dead; eternal judgment.
MSG.3: God helping us, we'll stay true to all that. But there's so much more. Let's get on with it!
MSG.4: Once people have seen the light, gotten a taste of heaven and been part of the work of the Holy Spirit,
MSG.5: once they've personally experienced the sheer goodness of God's Word and the powers breaking in on us—
MSG.6: if then they turn their backs on it, washing their hands of the whole thing, well, they can't start over as if nothing happened. That's impossible. Why, they've re-crucified Jesus! They've repudiated him in public!
MSG.7: Parched ground that soaks up the rain and then produces an abundance of carrots and corn for its gardener gets God's "Well done!"
MSG.8: But if it produces weeds and thistles, it's more likely to get cussed out. Fields like that are burned, not harvested.
MSG.9: I'm sure that won't happen to you, friends. I have better things in mind for you—salvation things!
MSG.10: God doesn't miss anything. He knows perfectly well all the love you've shown him by helping needy Christians, and that you keep at it.
MSG.11: And now I want each of you to extend that same intensity toward a full-bodied hope, and keep at it till the finish.
MSG.12: Don't drag your feet. Be like those who stay the course with committed faith and then get everything promised to them.
MSG.13: When God made his promise to Abraham, he backed it to the hilt, putting his own reputation on the line.
MSG.14: He said, "I promise that I'll bless you with everything I have—bless and bless and bless!"
MSG.15: Abraham stuck it out and got everything that had been promised to him.
MSG.16: When people make promises, they guarantee them by appeal to some authority above them so that if there is any question that they'll make good on the promise, the authority will back them up.
MSG.17: When God wanted to guarantee his promises, he gave his word, a rock-solid guarantee—
MSG.18: God can't break his word. And because his word cannot change, the promise is likewise unchangeable. We who have run for our very lives to God have every reason to grab the promised hope with both hands and never let go.
MSG.19: It's an unbreakable spiritual lifeline, reaching past all appearances right to the very presence of God
MSG.20: where Jesus, running on ahead of us, has taken up his permanent post as high priest for us, in the order of Melchizedek.

NIV.1: Therefore let us leave the elementary teachings about Christ and go on to maturity, not laying again the foundation of repentance from acts that lead to death, and of faith in God,
NIV.2: instruction about baptisms, the laying on of hands, the resurrection of the dead, and eternal judgment.
NIV.3: And God permitting, we will do so.
NIV.4: It is impossible for those who have once been enlightened, who have tasted the heavenly gift, who have shared in the Holy Spirit,
NIV.5: who have tasted the goodness of the word of God and the powers of the coming age,
NIV.6: if they fall away, to be brought back to repentance, because to their loss they are crucifying the Son of God all over again and subjecting him to public disgrace.
NIV.7: Land that drinks in the rain often falling on it and that produces a crop useful to those for whom it is farmed receives the blessing of God.
NIV.8: But land that produces thorns and thistles is worthless and is in danger of being cursed. In the end it will be burned.
NIV.9: Even though we speak like this, dear friends, we are confident of better things in your case—things that accompany salvation.
NIV.10: God is not unjust; he will not forget your work and the love you have shown him as you have helped his people and continue to help them.
NIV.11: We want each of you to show this same diligence to the very end, in order to make your hope sure.
NIV.12: We do not want you to become lazy, but to imitate those who through faith and patience inherit what has been promised.
NIV.13:
The Certainty of God’s Promise
When God made his promise to Abraham, since there was no one greater for him to swear by, he swore by himself,
NIV.14: saying, “I will surely bless you and give you many descendants.”
NIV.15: And so after waiting patiently, Abraham received what was promised.
NIV.16: Men swear by someone greater than themselves, and the oath confirms what is said and puts an end to all argument.
NIV.17: Because God wanted to make the unchanging nature of his purpose very clear to the heirs of what was promised, he confirmed it with an oath.
NIV.18: God did this so that, by two unchangeable things in which it is impossible for God to lie, we who have fled to take hold of the hope offered to us may be greatly encouraged.
NIV.19: We have this hope as an anchor for the soul, firm and secure. It enters the inner sanctuary behind the curtain,
NIV.20: where Jesus, who went before us, has entered on our behalf. He has become a high priest forever, in the order of Melchizedek.

NRSVCE.1: Therefore let us go on toward perfection, leaving behind the basic teaching about Christ, and not laying again the foundation: repentance from dead works and faith toward God,
NRSVCE.2: instruction about baptisms, laying on of hands, resurrection of the dead, and eternal judgment.
NRSVCE.3: And we will do this, if God permits.
NRSVCE.4: For it is impossible to restore again to repentance those who have once been enlightened, and have tasted the heavenly gift, and have shared in the Holy Spirit,
NRSVCE.5: and have tasted the goodness of the word of God and the powers of the age to come,
NRSVCE.6: and then have fallen away, since on their own they are crucifying again the Son of God and are holding him up to contempt.
NRSVCE.7: Ground that drinks up the rain falling on it repeatedly, and that produces a crop useful to those for whom it is cultivated, receives a blessing from God.
NRSVCE.8: But if it produces thorns and thistles, it is worthless and on the verge of being cursed; its end is to be burned over.
NRSVCE.9: Even though we speak in this way, beloved, we are confident of better things in your case, things that belong to salvation.
NRSVCE.10: For God is not unjust; he will not overlook your work and the love that you showed for his sake in serving the saints, as you still do.
NRSVCE.11: And we want each one of you to show the same diligence so as to realize the full assurance of hope to the very end,
NRSVCE.12: so that you may not become sluggish, but imitators of those who through faith and patience inherit the promises.
NRSVCE.13: When God made a promise to Abraham, because he had no one greater by whom to swear, he swore by himself,
NRSVCE.14: saying, “I will surely bless you and multiply you.”
NRSVCE.15: And thus Abraham, having patiently endured, obtained the promise.
NRSVCE.16: Human beings, of course, swear by someone greater than themselves, and an oath given as confirmation puts an end to all dispute.
NRSVCE.17: In the same way, when God desired to show even more clearly to the heirs of the promise the unchangeable character of his purpose, he guaranteed it by an oath,
NRSVCE.18: so that through two unchangeable things, in which it is impossible that God would prove false, we who have taken refuge might be strongly encouraged to seize the hope set before us.
NRSVCE.19: We have this hope, a sure and steadfast anchor of the soul, a hope that enters the inner shrine behind the curtain,
NRSVCE.20: where Jesus, a forerunner on our behalf, has entered, having become a high priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek.

NET.1: Therefore we must progress beyond the elementary instructions about Christ and move on to maturity, not laying this foundation again: repentance from dead works and faith in God,
NET.2: teaching about baptisms, laying on of hands, resurrection of the dead, and eternal judgment.
NET.3: And this is what we intend to do, if God permits.
NET.4: For it is impossible in the case of those who have once been enlightened, tasted the heavenly gift, become partakers of the Holy Spirit,
NET.5: tasted the good word of God and the miracles of the coming age,
NET.6: and then have committed apostasy, to renew them again to repentance, since they are crucifying the Son of God for themselves all over again and holding him up to contempt.
NET.7: For the ground that has soaked up the rain that frequently falls on it and yields useful vegetation for those who tend it receives a blessing from God.
NET.8: But if it produces thorns and thistles, it is useless and about to be cursed; its fate is to be burned.
NET.9: But in your case, dear friends, even though we speak like this, we are convinced of better things relating to salvation.
NET.10: For God is not unjust so as to forget your work and the love you have demonstrated for his name, in having served and continuing to serve the saints.
NET.11: But we passionately want each of you to demonstrate the same eagerness for the fulfillment of your hope until the end,
NET.12: so that you may not be sluggish, but imitators of those who through faith and perseverance inherit the promises.
NET.13: Now when God made his promise to Abraham, since he could swear by no one greater, he swore by himself,
NET.14: saying, “Surely I will bless you greatly and multiply your descendants abundantly.”
NET.15: And so by persevering, Abraham inherited the promise.
NET.16: For people swear by something greater than themselves, and the oath serves as a confirmation to end all dispute.
NET.17: In the same way God wanted to demonstrate more clearly to the heirs of the promise that his purpose was unchangeable, and so he intervened with an oath,
NET.18: so that we who have found refuge in him may find strong encouragement to hold fast to the hope set before us through two unchangeable things, since it is impossible for God to lie.
NET.19: We have this hope as an anchor for the soul, sure and steadfast, which reaches inside behind the curtain,
NET.20: where Jesus our forerunner entered on our behalf, since he became a priest forever in the order of Melchizedek.
